title: Closures, Lexical State, Currying, and Factories · Mastery Check
summary: Create configurable validators without mutable module globals or late-binding bugs. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a closure-based validator factory with capture tests. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace lexical name capture and cell lookup after the enclosing call returns.
Detect and repair late-bound loop variables in generated callables.
Distinguish deliberate nonlocal state from pure closure configuration and document the effect.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Closure Capture value-and-effect pipeline for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Closure Capture assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Create two validators with thresholds 25 and 30. Their captured settings must remain isolated across repeated calls, and wrappers must preserve names, signatures, returns, and exceptions.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Closure Capture has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Closure Capture precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Closures copy every captured value at definition time.

options.text: Separate immutable values from observable effects and ownership boundaries

Compose and run the smallest pipeline while retaining an inspectable trace
Repeat with changed input, verify input preservation, and move effects to the shell
